summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike on the night of 15 June, targeting Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv with 70 rockets and 582 drones, of which 632 were shot down. Multiple guided aviation bomb strikes hit Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, while cruise missiles were launched from Russian territories toward Chernihiv, Kyiv and Sumy. UAV swarms continue to operate over numerous northern and central Ukrainian oblasts, threatening both civilian and infrastructure targets.
key_points: A combined strike on 15 June involved 70 rockets and 582 attack drones, primarily aimed at Kyiv, with additional attacks on Dnipro and Kharkiv, and 632 targets were intercepted (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Guided aviation bombs were deployed over Dnipropetrovsk (Synelnykivka district), Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @kpszsu/65250).
Cruise missiles were launched from Bryansk and Kursk regions toward Chernihiv and Kyiv, and from Tu‑160 aircraft (Source: @kpszsu/65219; @eRadarrua/96777).
UAV groups were reported moving from Pavlograd toward Dnipro and from the Black Sea toward Odesa (Source: @kpszsu/65249; @kpszsu/65253).
Railway station Lozova in Kharkiv region was hit by UAVs, damaging locomotives and injuring two crew members (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Power outages affected Kyiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, Odesa and Zaporizhzhia after rocket and drone strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81936).
Multiple UAV swarms were observed over northern oblasts, including routes from Chernihiv to Kyiv and from Sumy toward Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/65221).

sections.executive_summary: A massive combined airstrike on the night of 15 June saw 70 rockets and 582 attack drones launched, primarily targeting Kyiv and also striking Dnipro and Kharkiv, with Ukrainian defenses downing 632 of these threats. Guided aviation bombs were simultaneously employed across Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, while cruise missiles were fired from Russian territories toward Chernihiv, Kyiv and Sumy. UAV swarms continue to operate over numerous northern and central oblasts, threatening both civilian populations and critical infrastructure.

points: Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 632 aerial targets during the night of 15 June, including 50 rockets and 582 drones of various types (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Railway station Lozova in Kharkiv region suffered UAV attacks that damaged locomotives and injured the driver and assistant (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Power outages were reported in Kyiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, Odesa and Zaporizhzhia following rocket and drone strikes, prompting emergency restoration efforts (Source: @UkraineNow/81936).
UAV groups were observed moving from the north of Chernihiv oblast toward Kyiv, and from the north of Sumy oblast toward Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/65221).
A reactive UAV launched from the Black Sea was heading toward the Odesa region, specifically Chornomorsk/Odesa (Source: @kpszsu/65253).

Russian forces launched 70 rockets, including 6 3M22 "Cirkon" anti‑ship missiles, 34 Iskander‑M/S‑400 ballistic missiles and 30 Kh‑101/Iskander‑K cruise missiles, from launch areas in Crimea, Bryansk, Kursk, Vologda and other regions (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Cruise missiles were fired from Tu‑160 strategic bombers, as noted in recent reports (Source: @eRadarrua/96777).
Guided aviation bombs were deployed over Dnipropetrovsk (Synelnykivka district), Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, indicating use of tactical air assets (Source: @kpszsu/65250).
UAV swarms were launched from the north of Chernihiv oblast heading south toward Kyiv, and from the south of Cherkasy oblast heading north, showing multi‑directional pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65228).
Additional UAV activity was reported near Pavlograd moving toward Dnipro, suggesting coordinated strikes from multiple axes (Source: @kpszsu/65249).
Multiple cruise missile groups were directed toward Chernihiv and its suburbs, increasing pressure on the northeastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/96756).
UAV clouds were observed near Knyazhichi moving into Kyiv districts Rembaza, Darnitsa and Bortnychi, indicating a threat to the capital from the east (Source: @eRadarrua/96775).
UAV groups were reported east of Nizhyn moving southward, potentially targeting the central‑eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65221).
Guided aviation bomb strikes were recorded on Kharkiv region, adding to the aerial threat in the east (Source: @kpszsu/65238).
A UAV swarm was noted over the area between Bashankа and Yelaneц (Mikolayivshchyna) moving northwest, suggesting activity near the eastern border (Source: @kpszsu/65221).
Rockets launched from Bryansk region passed through Chernihiv oblast toward Kyiv, highlighting a northern approach (Source: @kpszsu/65219).
UAVs were observed from the north of Chernihiv heading directly to Kyiv, increasing the risk to the capital from the north (Source: @kpszsu/65221).
UAVs on the north of Sumy oblast were moving toward Chernihiv, indicating coordinated northern operations (Source: @kpszsu/65221).
Additional rockets were reported heading to Kyiv from the north‑east, reinforcing northern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65217).
UAV activity was noted on the north of Kirovohrad oblast moving toward Kropyvnytskyi, showing a western‑northern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65221).
Rockets were launched toward Cherkasy from the south and east, with some changing course northward, indicating southern pressure on central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65228).
Guided aviation bombs were deployed over Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, reflecting southern‑eastern strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/65242).
UAVs on the border of Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk were heading north, suggesting a southern launch axis (Source: @kpszsu/65242).
A UAV group moved from Pavlograd toward Dnipro, a southern‑central corridor (Source: @kpszsu/65249).
UAV attacks on Putyvl in Sumy region caused fires and a second strike on rescue crews, showing southern‑northern spillover (Source: @UkraineNow/81914).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 632 aerial targets on 15 June, including 50 rockets and 582 drones of various types (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
MiG‑31K aircraft sorties were announced as possible for the day, indicating heightened air patrol activity (Source: @eRadarrua/96813).
Russian cruise missile launches from Tu‑160 bombers were reported, expanding the long‑range strike envelope (Source: @eRadarrua/96777).
Guided aviation bomb strikes were recorded across multiple oblasts, showing use of tactical air assets (Source: @kpszsu/65250).
UAV swarms were observed over numerous districts in Kyiv, including Shulyavka, Obolon, Troian, Voskresenka and Darnitsa, reflecting dense low‑altitude activity (Source: @eRadarrua/96787).

key_developments: Massive combined airstrike on 15 June: 70 rockets and 582 attack drones targeted Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv; Ukrainian defenses shot down 632 threats (Source: @kpszsu/65248).
Guided aviation bomb strikes hit Dnipropetrovsk (Synelnykivka), Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, indicating expanded tactical air use (Source: @kpszsu/65250).

Cruise missiles launched from Bryansk and Kursk regions toward Chernihiv and Kyiv, plus Tu‑160‑borne missiles, show long‑range pressure from Russian territory (Source: @kpszsu/65219; @eRadarrua/96777).
UAV swarms operating from the north of Chernihiv and Sumy toward Kyiv increase the risk of low‑altitude attacks on the capital (Source: @kpszsu/65221).
Railway station Lozova in Kharkiv region was hit by UAVs, damaging locomotives and injuring crew members (Source: @UkraineNow/81912).
Power outages across Kyiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, Odesa and Zaporizhzhia after rocket and drone strikes highlight infrastructure vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/81936).
Reactive UAV from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region demonstrates maritime‑origin drone threats (Source: @kpszsu/65253).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air operations have intensified, combining high‑altitude missile strikes with low‑altitude UAV swarms across multiple fronts. The use of guided aviation bombs and cruise missiles from strategic bombers expands the strike depth, while dense UAV activity over northern and central oblasts threatens both civilian areas and critical infrastructure. Ukrainian air defenses remain effective but face sustained pressure, requiring continued allocation of interceptor assets and radar coverage.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have mobilized air defense units, radar networks and electronic warfare teams to counter the surge in aerial threats, as evidenced by the high interception count on 15 June. Regional emergency services are responding to infrastructure damage, including railway repairs and power restoration in affected oblasts. Coordination with allied intelligence sources continues to track missile launch sites and UAV trajectories for pre‑emptive alerts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring indicates increased launch activity from Russian regions of Bryansk, Kursk, Vologda and Crimea, suggesting a coordinated multi‑axis offensive. UAV flight paths reveal a pattern of north‑to‑south movement targeting Kyiv and surrounding districts, while maritime‑origin drones are emerging from the Black Sea corridor. Continued observation of guided bomb deployments will be essential to anticipate future tactical air strikes.
